children.heading: Coordination
children.label: a Coordination
children.text: The President, through the National Science and Technology Council, shall coordinate and support a national research program to improve understanding of the role of the oceans in human health.

establish, for the 10-year period beginning in the year it is submitted, the goals and priorities for Federal research which most effectively advance scientific understanding of the connections between the oceans and human health, provide usable information for the prediction of marine-related public health problems and use the biological potential of the oceans for development of new treatments of human diseases and a greater understanding of human biology;

describe specific activities required to achieve such goals and priorities, including the funding of competitive research grants, ocean and coastal observations, training and support for scientists, and participation in international research efforts;
identify and address, as appropriate, relevant programs and activities of the Federal agencies and departments that would contribute to the program;
identify alternatives for preventive unnecessary duplication of effort among Federal agencies and departments with respect to the program;

consider and use, as appropriate, reports and studies conducted by Federal agencies and departments, the National Research Council, the Ocean Research Advisory Panel, the Commission on Ocean Policy and other expert scientific bodies;
make recommendations for the coordination of program activities with ocean and human health-related activities of other national and international organizations; and
estimate Federal funding for research activities to be conducted under the program.

children.label: A
children.text: vector- and water-borne diseases of humans and marine organisms, including marine mammals and fish;

children.label: B
children.text: harmful algal blooms and hypoxia (through the Inter-Agency Task Force on Harmful Algal Blooms and Hypoxia);

children.label: C
children.text: marine-derived pharmaceuticals;

children.label: D
children.text: marine organisms as models for biomedical research and as indicators of marine environmental health;

children.label: E
children.text: marine environmental microbiology;

children.label: F
children.text: bioaccumulative and endocrine-disrupting chemical contaminants; and

children.label: G
children.text: predictive models based on indicators of marine environmental health or public health threats.

Coordination with the National Ocean Research Leadership Council () to ensure that any integrated ocean and coastal observing system provides information necessary to monitor and reduce marine public health problems including health-related data on biological populations and detection of contaminants in marine waters and seafood.10 U.S.C. 8932(a)1

children.label: A
children.text: genomics and proteomics to develop genetic and immunological detection approaches and predictive tools and to discover new biomedical resources;

children.label: B
children.text: biomaterials and bioengineering;

children.label: C
children.text: in situ and remote sensors used to detect, quantify, and predict the presence and spread of contaminants in marine waters and organisms and to identify new genetic resources for biomedical purposes;

children.label: D
children.text: techniques for supplying marine resources, including chemical synthesis, culturing and aquaculturing marine organisms, new fermentation methods and recombinant techniques; and

children.label: E
children.text: adaptation of equipment and technologies from human health fields.
Support for scholars, trainees and education opportunities that encourage an interdisciplinary and international approach to exploring the diversity of life in the oceans.
a summary of the achievements of Federal oceans and human health research, including Federally supported external research, during the preceding fiscal year;
an analysis of the progress made toward achieving the goals and objectives of the plan developed under subsection (b), including identification of trends and emerging trends;
a copy or summary of the plan and any changes made in the plan;
a summary of agency budgets for oceans and human health activities for that preceding fiscal year; and
any recommendations regarding additional action or legislation that may be required to assist in achieving the purposes of this chapter.
